# Extractor Receipt

## Summary

This source contains framework-design evidence, benchmark results, ablation results, efficiency-extension results, and explicit limitation statements for a long-horizon LLM-agent architecture focused on context management and strategic oversight.

## Evidence Files

### file: evidence-01.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-10-005
evidence_id: evidence-01
kind: method
citation: Wan et al., 2025, COMPASS
source_url: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2510.08790
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: direct
contradiction_relevance: none
---
COMPASS is a hierarchical framework with three specialized components: a MainAgent, a Meta-Thinker, and a ContextManager.

## Source Excerpt

"We propose COMPASS (Context-Organized Multi-Agent Planning and Strategy System), a lightweight hierarchical framework that separates tactical execution, strategic oversight, and context organization into three specialized components: (1) a MainAgent ... (2) a Meta-Thinker ... and (3) a ContextManager ..."

## Note

The paper presents this as the core architectural decomposition.
```

### file: evidence-02.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-10-005
evidence_id: evidence-02
kind: claim-support
citation: Wan et al., 2025, COMPASS
source_url: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2510.08790
confidence: medium
mechanism_relevance: direct
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The paper identifies context management as the central bottleneck in long-horizon agent reasoning.

## Source Excerpt

"We identify context management as the central bottleneck—extended histories cause agents to overlook critical evidence or become distracted by irrelevant information, thus failing to replan or reflect from previous mistakes."

## Note

This is the authors' framing of the main failure source for long-horizon tasks.
```

### file: evidence-03.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-10-005
evidence_id: evidence-03
kind: result
citation: Wan et al., 2025, COMPASS
source_url: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2510.08790
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Across GAIA, BrowseComp, and Humanity's Last Exam, the paper reports accuracy gains of up to 20% relative to single-agent and multi-agent baselines.

## Source Excerpt

"Across three challenging benchmarks—GAIA, BrowseComp, and Humanity's Last Exam—COMPASS improves accuracy by up to 20% relative to both single- and multi-agent baselines."

## Note

This is a headline aggregate performance claim rather than a single table row.
```

### file: evidence-04.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-10-005
evidence_id: evidence-04
kind: metric
citation: Wan et al., 2025, Table 2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2510.08790
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
With Gemini 2.5 Pro on BrowseComp, COMPASS scores 35.4 Pass@1.

## Source Excerpt

In Table 2 under "Gemini 2.5 Pro," the COMPASS row reports BrowseComp = "35.4".

## Note

This is the reported Pass@1 value for that model-benchmark pairing.
```

### file: evidence-05.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-10-005
evidence_id: evidence-05
kind: metric
citation: Wan et al., 2025, Table 2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2510.08790
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
With Gemini 2.5 Pro on GAIA and HLE, COMPASS scores 67.8 and 31.7 Pass@1, respectively.

## Source Excerpt

In Table 2 under "Gemini 2.5 Pro," the COMPASS row reports GAIA = "67.8" and HLE = "31.7".

## Note

These are the reported GAIA and HLE Pass@1 values for base COMPASS with Gemini 2.5 Pro.
```

### file: evidence-06.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-10-005
evidence_id: evidence-06
kind: result
citation: Wan et al., 2025, Table 2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2510.08790
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Adding test-time scaling raises COMPASS to 43.7 on BrowseComp, 72.1 on GAIA, and 35.2 on HLE with Gemini 2.5 Pro.

## Source Excerpt

In Table 2, "COMPASS-TTS (Gemini 2.5 Pro)" reports BrowseComp = "43.7", GAIA = "72.1", and HLE = "35.2".

## Note

This evidence is about the scaled variant, not the base COMPASS system.
```

### file: evidence-07.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-10-005
evidence_id: evidence-07
kind: metric
citation: Wan et al., 2025, Table 3
source_url: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2510.08790
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Removing the Meta-Thinker drops BrowseComp Pass@1 from 35.4 in the full system to 15.2.

## Source Excerpt

Table 3 lists the full system at "35.4" Pass@1, while the Meta-Thinking ablation "None (Main + Context only)" reports "15.2".

## Note

This supports the contribution of the Meta-Thinker through ablation, but it does not by itself isolate all causal pathways beyond the altered component.
```

### file: evidence-08.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-10-005
evidence_id: evidence-08
kind: metric
citation: Wan et al., 2025, Table 3
source_url: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2510.08790
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Removing the ContextManager drops BrowseComp Pass@1 from 35.4 in the full system to 26.4.

## Source Excerpt

Table 3 lists the full system at "35.4" Pass@1, while the ContextManager ablation "None (No ContextManager)" reports "26.4".

## Note

This is ablation evidence for the ContextManager's contribution; it shows performance loss under removal but does not fully specify why the loss occurs.
```

### file: evidence-09.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-10-005
evidence_id: evidence-09
kind: result
citation: Wan et al., 2025, Section 5.1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2510.08790
confidence: medium
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The paper reports that Context-12B achieves performance comparable to Gemini 2.5 Flash on BrowseComp while using about 70% of the tokens.

## Source Excerpt

"As shown in Figure 4, we evaluated the performance of Context-12B on Browsecomp, achieving performance comparable to a larger models (Gemini 2.5 Flash) while using only 70% of their tokens with a SFT-DPO training pipeline."

## Note

This is a reported efficiency comparison from the paper's specialized context-manager extension.
```

### file: evidence-10.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-10-005
evidence_id: evidence-10
kind: claim-support
citation: Wan et al., 2025, Section 5.2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2510.08790
confidence: medium
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
In COMPASS-TTS, accuracy improves as the number of parallel samples increases, but token cost also rises, and gains plateau around n = 4.

## Source Excerpt

"Figure 5 plots accuracy and total tokens for n ∈ {1, 2, 4, 8}. All methods improve monotonically over n = 1, with Full-PS showing the strongest gains but steepest token growth, MT-PS offering a balanced middle ground, and CM-PS achieving the best efficiency. Performance plateaus around n = 4, suggesting that n = 2–4 offers a practical sweet spot to balance accuracy and costs."

## Note

This evidence includes an explicit cost-performance tradeoff, so the efficiency caveat should remain attached downstream.
```

### file: evidence-11.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-10-005
evidence_id: evidence-11
kind: claim-support
citation: Wan et al., 2025, Limitations
source_url: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2510.08790
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
The evaluation is limited to controlled settings centered on searching, text-based browsing, and code execution, and the study primarily uses proprietary frontier models.

## Source Excerpt

"While COMPASS demonstrates strong performance on QA-style agentic benchmarks, our evaluation currently emphasizes controlled reasoning environments..." The paper adds that experiments were conducted in settings involving "searching, text-based browsing, and code execution tools." It also states, "Our study primarily focuses on proprietary frontier models..."

## Note

This limitation bounds how far the reported results can be generalized to more open-ended domains or open-source model settings.
---

## Summary

This source contains framework-design evidence, benchmark results, ablation results, efficiency-extension results, and explicit limitation statements for a long-horizon LLM-agent architecture focused on context management and strategic oversight.

## Evidence Files

### file: evidence-01.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-10-005
evidence_id: evidence-01
kind: method
citation: Wan et al., 2025, COMPASS
source_url: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2510.08790
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: direct
contradiction_relevance: none
---
COMPASS is a hierarchical framework with three specialized components: a MainAgent, a Meta-Thinker, and a ContextManager.

## Source Excerpt

"We propose COMPASS (Context-Organized Multi-Agent Planning and Strategy System), a lightweight hierarchical framework that separates tactical execution, strategic oversight, and context organization into three specialized components: (1) a MainAgent ... (2) a Meta-Thinker ... and (3) a ContextManager ..."

## Note

The paper presents this as the core architectural decomposition.
```

```markdown
---

The paper identifies context management as the central bottleneck in long-horizon agent reasoning.

## Source Excerpt

"We identify context management as the central bottleneck—extended histories cause agents to overlook critical evidence or become distracted by irrelevant information, thus failing to replan or reflect from previous mistakes."

## Note

This is the authors' framing of the main failure source for long-horizon tasks.
```

### file: evidence-07.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-10-005

citation: Wan et al., 2025, Table 3
source_url: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2510.08790
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Removing the Meta-Thinker drops BrowseComp Pass@1 from 35.4 in the full system to 15.2.

## Source Excerpt

Table 3 lists the full system at "35.4" Pass@1, while the Meta-Thinking ablation "None (Main + Context only)" reports "15.2".

## Note

This supports the contribution of the Meta-Thinker through ablation, but it does not by itself isolate all causal pathways beyond the altered component.
```

The paper reports that Context-12B achieves performance comparable to Gemini 2.5 Flash on BrowseComp while using about 70% of the tokens.

## Source Excerpt

"As shown in Figure 4, we evaluated the performance of Context-12B on Browsecomp, achieving performance comparable to a larger models (Gemini 2.5 Flash) while using only 70% of their tokens with a SFT-DPO training pipeline."

## Note

This is a reported efficiency comparison from the paper's specialized context-manager extension.
```

### file: evidence-10.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-10-005
evidence_id: evidence-10
kind: claim-support
citation: Wan et al., 2025, Section 5.2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2510.08790
confidence: medium
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
In COMPASS-TTS, accuracy improves as the number of parallel samples increases, but token cost also rises, and gains plateau around n = 4.

## Source Excerpt

"Figure 5 plots accuracy and total tokens for n ∈ {1, 2, 4, 8}. All methods improve monotonically over n = 1, with Full-PS showing the strongest gains but steepest token growth, MT-PS offering a balanced middle ground, and CM-PS achieving the best efficiency. Performance plateaus around n = 4, suggesting that n = 2–4 offers a practical sweet spot to balance accuracy and costs."

## Note

This evidence includes an explicit cost-performance tradeoff, so the efficiency caveat should remain attached downstream.
```

### file: evidence-11.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-10-005
evidence_id: evidence-11
kind: claim-support
citation: Wan et al., 2025, Limitations
source_url: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2510.08790
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
The evaluation is limited to controlled settings centered on searching, text-based browsing, and code execution, and the study primarily uses proprietary frontier models.

## Source Excerpt

"While COMPASS demonstrates strong performance on QA-style agentic benchmarks, our evaluation currently emphasizes controlled reasoning environments..." The paper adds that experiments were conducted in settings involving "searching, text-based browsing, and code execution tools." It also states, "Our study primarily focuses on proprietary frontier models..."

## Note

This limitation bounds how far the reported results can be generalized to more open-ended domains or open-source model settings.
```
